name: Brent Crude
summary: Day 54 Tuesday AM. MONDAY CASH CLOSE $94.88 (+5.0% 1D, +46.1% SINCE FEB 28) — BELOW GOLDMAN $96-98 INTERIM BAND DESPITE 88% HORMUZ TRANSIT SUPPRESSION. Paper-physical divergence is the intelligence signal of the tape: operational reality at severe-case scenario (16 ships vs 138 baseline Monday + zero Sunday); paper priced as contained one-off. Floating-storage in Gulf + AWRP moderation (~1.0%) + Wednesday-binary pricing are suppressing the paper re-rate. Wednesday April 22 expiry resolves the asymmetry: ceasefire renewal → $91-93 band (Goldman base case + Brew Eurasia structural $92-95 floor); lapse → $100-105 within 48 hrs as transit data becomes binding (Morgan Stanley $110 Q2 / Goldman severe $115 Q4 activates). Brew Eurasia: Brent below $80 'very unlikely' at any point 2026 — ceasefire real economic effect was AWRP moderation, already achieved; incremental diplomatic progress = diminishing returns on Brent. Tuesday Globex ~$94.69-95.50 — paper still below Goldman $96-98, confirming non-convergence.

current.price: $92.48 (Wed 11:51 IST); overnight arc $95.48 Tue settle → $101.97 spike → $92.48

price_snapshots.trigger: Pre-war baseline
price_snapshots.delta_context: Last price before US-Israel strikes on Iran

price_snapshots.trigger: Initial war spike
price_snapshots.delta_context: Surged ~15% on first trading day after attacks

price_snapshots.trigger: Crossed $100 first time in 4 years
price_snapshots.delta_context: Hormuz closure effect fully priced

price_snapshots.trigger: South Pars + Ras Laffan attacks
price_snapshots.delta_context: Intraday peak on energy infrastructure escalation

price_snapshots.trigger: Iraq force majeure
price_snapshots.delta_context: Iraq cut production from 3.3M to 900K bpd

price_snapshots.trigger: Trump announced 5-day strike pause, claimed Iran talks
price_snapshots.delta_context: Fell from $112 to $103 in single session. Biggest single-day drop since war began.

price_snapshots.trigger: Rebound as Iran denies talks, strikes continue, Houthi escalation signal
price_snapshots.delta_context: Partial reversal of yesterday's crash. Market realizing the 'talks' may be fiction. Chevron CEO says prices 'not fully priced in.'

price_snapshots.trigger: Houthi war entry + Tangsiri assassination + toll booth + ceasefire rejection
price_snapshots.delta_context: Surged from $103.56 (Mar 24) to $112.57. Day 21+ above $100. Goldman estimates $14-18/bbl risk premium.

price_snapshots.trigger: First major pullback — Trump diplomacy claims + profit-taking
price_snapshots.delta_context: Dropped 4.3% from $112.57. First significant decline since war began. Built on claims not confirmed de-escalation. Pentagon preparing ground ops simultaneously.

price_snapshots.trigger: WHIPLASH: $107→$104→$116→$115 on Trump contradictory signals
price_snapshots.delta_context: Morning pullback on 'agreed to most' diplomacy claim reversed within hours by 'take the oil' FT interview. $12/bbl swing in 18 hours.

price_snapshots.trigger: PARADIGM SHIFT: Hormuz decoupling — WSJ reports Trump willing to end war without Hormuz reopening
price_snapshots.delta_context: Crashed 4.6% on WSJ report, recovered to ~$112.90 by session end. IEA 400M barrel SPR release began. Al-Salmi tanker attacked by Iranian drone at Dubai port. March closes as largest monthly Brent surge since 1988 inception.

price_snapshots.trigger: LARGEST single-session crash since war began — triple peace signal
price_snapshots.delta_context: Crashed -14.0% from $118.35 to $101.76 — surpassing March 23 (-8.4%). Triple driver: Pezeshkian peace signal, Trump '2-3 weeks', IEA SPR flowing. Testing $100 structural floor. Oil+equities moved same direction (unusual): Brent down on supply normalization hopes, equities up on peace hopes. XLE -1.1% while S&P +2.9% confirms rally is oil-driven. Hormuz decoupling floor holding: $101.76, not below $100.

price_snapshots.trigger: First breach below $100 since ~March 20 — structural floor test
price_snapshots.delta_context: Intraday $98.52 was first time below $100 in 13 days. Recovered to $101.97 close (-2% on day). Brent-WTI spread collapsed from $18-20 to ~$2-3. War premium evaporating but Hormuz NOT reopened, toll law formalized. Recovery from $98.52 suggests buying interest at $100.

price_snapshots.trigger: War premium RESTORED — largest single-session crude move of war
price_snapshots.delta_context: WTI +11.9% to $112.06 — most violent single-session move. Driven by Trump 'hit hard' address + pharmaceutical strikes + April 6 deadline pricing. F-15 shootdown adds military credibility gap. Oil-equity divergence at widest of war.

price_snapshots.trigger: WTI-Brent inversion + Tuesday deadline binary
price_snapshots.delta_context: WTI surged above Brent by $3.83 — market reclassifying Gulf oil as semi-stranded

price_snapshots.trigger: Trump-Iran 14-day ceasefire announcement — biggest single-day Brent fall since 1991 Gulf War
price_snapshots.delta_context: Crashed -13.5% from $110.80 in 12 hours; Heating Oil -18.2%, WTI -15.0%, RBOB -13.5%; war risk insurance unchanged ($10-14M/voyage)

price_snapshots.trigger: Ceasefire crash — biggest single-day drop since April 2020
price_snapshots.delta_context: -11.5% from $109.27; Goldman risk premium $14→$4-6; BUT physical Dated Brent still $120+ = $24 gap

price_snapshots.trigger: Day 2 bounce — ceasefire euphoria fading
price_snapshots.delta_context: +1.96% from $94.75 close; Lebanon exclusion + Hormuz re-closure driving partial reversal

price_snapshots.trigger: Ceasefire fracture bounce — Lebanon excluded, Hormuz re-closed
price_snapshots.delta_context: Rebounded from $94.47 ceasefire crash low to $98.60 in 24 hours as ceasefire fractured. WTI $99.51. Physical Dated Brent still $120+.

price_snapshots.trigger: US naval blockade of Iranian ports announced — Brent back above $100
price_snapshots.delta_context: Surged +7.4% from $95.20 Friday close. Largest move since April 8 ceasefire. 5-session sub-$100 count reset.

price_snapshots.trigger: PARTIAL REVERSAL of rhetoric-driven sell-off — afternoon creep-back toward $100
price_snapshots.delta_context: Initially fell from $103+ blockade spike toward $97-98 on Trump 'Iran reached out' claim (unverified), then crept back to $99.19 by evening close (-0.2% on day). Sell-off partially reversed — market remains nervous about Hormuz supply reality. Approaches $100 trigger again. Physical-futures gap near widest of crisis: Dated Brent $120+ vs futures $99 = $21 gap. Market pricing 'deal in days' — any disruption restores $105+ instantly.

price_snapshots.trigger: Second session below $100; Dated Brent physical-futures gap exploded to ~$49
price_snapshots.delta_context: Physical Dated Brent hit $144/bbl (peak Apr 7-11) while futures at $95. Energy equities pricing $85-90. Three-layer mispricing.

price_snapshots.trigger: Intraday reversal — morning -6.5% bottom, settle -3.3%; WTI diverged to $87.77 (-7.3%), Brent-WTI spread $8.39 widest since blockade
price_snapshots.delta_context: Morning brief captured futures at $92.92 on Lebanon ceasefire + Trump 'very close' rhetoric; afternoon recovery driven by Pakistani Shalamar tanker confirmed Hormuz transit (selective-transit regime intact) and US session opening. EIA -9.13M bbl bullish inventory IGNORED by deal-sentiment market. Dated Brent physical ~$132 unchanged (no Friday quote — Good Friday). Physical-futures gap NARROWED to ~$36 (from $39 morning) only because futures bounced — physical did not ease. WTI -7.3% reflects US domestic absorbing deal-optimism without blockade hedge; Brent held up by physical-market discipline.

price_snapshots.trigger: FM Araghchi X-post 'Hormuz completely open for remaining period of ceasefire' triggered largest single-day crash of crisis
price_snapshots.delta_context: CORRECTION of evening brief's stale CNN 4pm intraday mark. Actual full-session settle on yfinance continuous: $91.87 / -7.6% (wire-ICE $90.38 / -9.1% — probably June contract). Intraday low $87.94 (-11.5%). WTI $84.00 / -11.3%. Heating Oil $3.34 / -12.8% — LARGEST SINGLE-ASSET MOVE. RBOB $2.95 / -6.9%. Brent-WTI spread $7.87 (vs evening brief's $8.39 stale). Physical-futures gap WIDENS to ~$40 (Dated Brent $132 held vs futures $91) — widest of war. Trump then reiterated 'blockade in full force until peace deal' and Baghaei clarified 'reciprocal measures if blockade continues' — partial settle bounce from $87.94 intraday low to $91.87 reflects these conditionals. S&P 500 record close 7,126.06 on same catalyst.

price_snapshots.price: Sunday re-pricing catalyst (SNSC + kinetic broadening + MEA summons)
price_snapshots.trigger: Iran's SNSC formally ratified IRGC Hormuz closure and re-imposed $1/bbl loaded-tanker fees — civilian-apex body (chaired by Pezeshkian, outranks IRGC and FM by statute) endorsed what IRGC declared Saturday morning; invalidates Friday's Araghchi-as-structural-fix pricing
price_snapshots.delta_context: Sunday event-driven price-snapshot flagged ahead of Monday Globex open (03:30 IST). Markets closed Sunday IST; CME Globex is first venue at 22:00 GMT. Sunday probability distribution per markets.md: Full re-pricing $100+ (35%, Goldman severe-case), Base bounce $95-98 (45%, MS base-case), Muted $92-94 (15%), Continued unwind $90-92 (5%). Gap ceiling $100+ if Sunday-evening kinetic fires or US counter-action; floor $92-93 if Islamabad delegation travel confirmed overnight. Goldman-vs-MS falsifiable test binds on Monday's first 10 sessions: Goldman right if Brent opens within 2% of $91.87 (paper treats kinetic as one-off); MS right if Brent gaps up 3%+ on Sunday Globex. Physical-futures gap ~$40 widest of crisis at Friday close — Sunday intel forces paper to converge upward.

price_snapshots.price: Sunday Globex indicatively ~$98 (June Brent) — +8.4% over Friday wire $90.38
price_snapshots.trigger: Morning +5-8% re-open thesis vindicated at upper edge by Sunday Globex; Sunday quiet provides CEILING (no new kinetic needed for tail $100+)
price_snapshots.delta_context: Paper-physical convergence direction confirmed — paper rising to physical (Dated ~$132). Lloyd's AWRP republish Monday London open is next discriminator: >=1.5% -> $100+; 1.0-1.2% -> $96-98 consolidation.

price_snapshots.price: ~$95.42 Monday Asia intraday; Sunday Globex $98 indicative
price_snapshots.trigger: Touska seizure + SNSC ratification + IRNA Round 2 rejection + Bessent Iranian waiver elapsed + Saturday IRGC kinetic broadening
price_snapshots.delta_context: +7.9% vs Friday wire $90.38 (+3.9% vs yfinance $91.87). Breaches Goldman one-off ceiling; below MS $100 full-convergence trigger. Interim mixed-conviction settle.

price_snapshots.trigger: Monday cash settle below Goldman $96-98 interim band despite 88% Hormuz transit suppression (16 vs 138 baseline) + Sunday zero-transit + Trump 'blockade stays'
price_snapshots.delta_context: Paper-physical divergence locks in as the intelligence signal of the tape — operational reality at severe-case scenario, paper priced as contained one-off. Floating-storage in Gulf + AWRP moderation (~1.0%) + Wednesday-binary pricing suppressing paper re-rate. Tuesday Globex $94.69-95.50 confirms non-convergence. Goldman-vs-MS test remains unresolved: Goldman $90 base case tracks if ceasefire renews (retrace to $91-93); Morgan Stanley $110 Q2 activates within 48 hrs of Wednesday lapse. Brew Eurasia structural floor $92-95 holds absent full diplomatic resolution.

signals.content: Oil surged faster than during any other conflict in recent history following US-Israel strikes on Iran and Iranian retaliation across Gulf states.
Brent crossed $100/bbl for first time in four years as Hormuz closure entered second week with no resolution in sight.
Crude topped $112 after Iraq declared force majeure on all foreign-operated oilfields and drones struck two Kuwaiti refineries. Fifth straight weekly gain.
US temporarily lifted some sanctions on Iranian oil — Treasury Sec Bessent said expected to add ~140M barrels to global market. Major policy reversal suggesting price sensitivity.

Brent crashed 8.4% to $102.73/bbl after Trump announced 5-day postponement of strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure, citing 'productive conversations' with Iran. Biggest single-day drop since war began. Iran denied any talks.

Brent rebounded 3.6% to $103.56 as market digested Iran's categorical denial of any talks, continued missile strikes on Israel, and Houthi announcement of entering the war threatening Red Sea closure. Chevron CEO Mike Wirth said effects are 'not fully priced in.' VIX rose 4.9% to 27.43, signaling returning fear. The rebound suggests yesterday's de-escalation optimism was premature.

Brent surged to $112.57 (+4.2% on day, +59.1% monthly). Multiple drivers: Iran ceasefire rejection (Mar 25), Tangsiri assassination (Mar 26), IRGC toll booth formalization (Mar 27), Houthi war entry (Mar 28). Goldman estimates $14-18/bbl geopolitical risk premium. Oil in steep backwardation. Day 21+ above $100.

Brent pulled back 4.3% to $107.71 — first major decline since war began. Driven by: (1) Trump claim Iran 'agreed to most of' 15-point demands, (2) Islamabad quad with Pakistan offering to host US-Iran talks, (3) profit-taking near $119 peak. Pullback is built on CLAIMS, not confirmed de-escalation. Pentagon simultaneously preparing Kharg Island ground ops. If Iran denies or ground ops proceed, pullback reverses. (Sources: Investing.com, CNN, Bloomberg, NPR)

EVENING UPDATE: Morning pullback to $107.71 was completely reversed. Trump's FT interview — 'my favourite thing is to take the oil in Iran' and Kharg Island seizure talk — drove Brent from $104 to $116 intraday, settling ~$115.27 (+2.4% from Friday $112.57). CNN headlined 'Oil prices top $116.' Additionally, Iran struck Kuwait desalination plant and Saudi intercepted 5 missiles — compounding supply fear. Brent +51% in March — on track for largest monthly surge on record. IEA: 'biggest oil shock in history.' The 18-hour arc (diplomacy claim → oil seizure rhetoric) encapsulates why claims-based oil moves are inherently fragile. (Sources: CNN Business, CNBC, Bloomberg, FT, Al Jazeera)

MORNING UPDATE: Brent at ~$115.27 (corrected from yfinance $107.35 which captured pre-FT-interview pricing). March closes as largest monthly surge in Brent history (+51-59%). IEA: 'biggest oil shock in history.' Trump's 'obliterate' Truth Social post adds further upward risk. Day 23+ above $100. Iran's selective Hormuz opening (China, Russia, India, Iraq, Pakistan, Malaysia, Thailand approved) does NOT resolve crisis — 95% below pre-war baseline. India markets closed (Mahavir Jayanti) — no onshore oil demand pricing today. GIFT Nifty -1.84% suggests April 1 reopening will price $115 oil negatively. April 6 energy pause deadline creates binary risk: deal or Brent $120+.

EVENING UPDATE — PARADIGM SHIFT: Brent crashed 4.6% to $107.60 on WSJ report that Trump told aides he's willing to end war even if Hormuz remains largely shut — the 'Hormuz decoupling' paradigm shift. Leavitt confirmed Hormuz is 'not a core objective.' This breaks the assumption that war resolution = Hormuz reopening = oil normalization. If war ends but Hormuz stays under Iranian selective control, the supply disruption is STRUCTURAL ($90-100 floor). Brent recovered to ~$112.90 by end of session. IEA 400M barrel coordinated SPR release (largest in history) began flowing. OPEC+ confirmed no output increase before Q3. Al-Salmi tanker attacked by Iranian drone at Dubai port. March closes as largest monthly Brent surge since 1988 inception (+38-55%).

Brent crashed -14.0% to $101.76 — LARGEST single-session decline since war began, surpassing March 23 (-8.4%). Triple driver: (1) Iran's President Pezeshkian told EU he has 'necessary will' to end war with 5-point counterproposal, (2) Trump said US could leave Iran 'in 2-3 weeks', (3) IEA 400M barrel SPR flowing at 1.0-1.5M bpd. Both sides signaling off-ramps within hours = market read coordinated de-escalation. XLE -1.1% while S&P +2.9% — explicit market signal that rally is oil-driven (bad for producers, good for economy). WSJ/CNN Hormuz decoupling now market consensus: even with ceasefire, $90-100 structural floor. Iran parliament drafting toll legislation. Brent landed at $101.76, NOT below $100 — the structural floor is holding. OPEC+ April 5 decides May levels; 206K bpd increase confirmed for April. Reversibility HIGH — March 23 saw -8.4% → full reversal in 5 days. The test: if Brent holds above $100 tomorrow, floor confirmed. If it breaks $100, paradigm shift. RBOB gasoline -9.5% confirms downstream following crude. (Sources: Bloomberg, Yahoo Finance, WSJ, CNN, DOE, CNBC)

Brent continued sliding to $101.97 (-2% on day) after yesterday's -10.5% crash, with intraday low of $98.52 — first breach below $100 since ~March 20 (13 days above). Triple de-escalation driver persists: Pezeshkian peace signals, Trump '2-3 weeks' timeline, SPR flowing at 1.0-1.5M bpd. Goldman previously described $100 as 'floor, not ceiling.' Recovery from $98.52 to $101.97 close suggests buying interest at $100. This is WAR PREMIUM EVAPORATION, not supply normalization — Hormuz NOT reopened, Iran's toll law passed full parliament.

Brent-WTI spread collapsed from $18-20 to ~$2-3 — market saying 'we no longer believe Gulf supply permanently disrupted.' WTI rose +2.4% driven by US stockpile build (+13.4M barrels, largest since Nov 2023). If Brent-WTI spread widens again, de-escalation thesis is failing and Gulf-specific premium returning.

April 5-8 creates extreme binary outcome risk: April 5 = OPEC+ meeting (206K bpd increase confirmed for April), April 6 = US energy strike pause expires, April 6-8 = RBI MPC. Three binary events in 4 days. If April 6 deadline expires without extension, Brent spikes to $110-120 within hours. March 23's -8.4% crash fully reversed in 5 days — same reversal risk present here.

POST-ADDRESS REVERSAL: Brent surged +8.3% to $109.57 after Trump's primetime address vowing to 'hit Iran extremely hard' and 'send them to the stone ages.' This is WAR PREMIUM RESTORATION, not new supply disruption — Hormuz status unchanged. The April 1 peace rally ($118→$101) was largely reversed. Market heard 'war continues, possibly escalates' louder than '2-3 weeks to end.' WTI surged +9.2% to $109.30. Intraday arc: $98.52 (first $100 breach in 13 days) → $101.97 regular close → $109.57 futures (address reaction). If April 6 deadline expires without extension, Brent spikes to $115-120 within hours. (Sources: CNBC, CNN, Bloomberg, yfinance, Fortune)

WTI surged +11.9% to $112.06 — largest single-session crude move since war began. Brent +7.8% to $109.05. Driven by Trump 'hit them extremely hard' primetime address + Iran pharmaceutical/port strikes escalating + April 6 energy pause deadline 3 days away with no extension signaled. The market is pricing maximum escalation, not peace. S&P 500 recovered to +0.1% on April 2 despite the oil surge — creating the key divergence: oil pricing escalation, equities pricing 2-3 week end. One is wrong. Heating Oil +9.2%, Frontline tankers +5.1%. OPEC+ April 5 meeting is 'most consequential since formation.' April 5-8 = densest event cluster of war (OPEC+, energy pause, RBI MPC, helium window). (Sources: CNBC, yfinance, Fortune, Bloomberg)

Weekend gap risk: April 4 Good Friday, April 5 Saturday. No settlement trading. Last close April 3: Brent $109.05 (+7.8%), WTI $112.06 (+11.9% — largest single-session move of war). War premium RESTORED after the $98.52→$109+ round-trip. F-15 shot down April 3 = war harder than expected. Trump 48-hour ultimatum + 'open the f***in' Strait.' Iran rejected: 'gates of hell.' April 6 deadline TOMORROW with zero extension signal. Oil pricing maximum escalation. S&P at 6,583 pricing peace. The divergence forces resolution April 5-8. Brent-WTI spread collapsed from $18-20 to ~$3 = Gulf-specific premium evaporating but Hormuz NOT reopened. Goldman '$100 floor' thesis validated by $98.52 bounce. (Sources: yfinance, Fortune, Bloomberg, CNBC, Al Jazeera)

Brent futures at $109.03, WTI $112.06 (pre-market April 6). Deadline EXTENDED 20 hours to Tuesday April 7. OPEC+ confirmed 206K bpd increase for May — same as April, representing <2% of ~17-20M bpd disrupted by Hormuz closure. OPEC+ warned 'restoring damaged energy assets to full capacity is costly and takes a long time.' The increase signals READINESS to flood once Hormuz reopens but zero incremental barrels today. Net effect on current oil: NEUTRAL. The REAL signal: OPEC+ pre-committed to pump post-ceasefire — post-war oil unwind FASTER and DEEPER than market expects. Oil-equity divergence persists: oil at $109-112 pricing maximum disruption, S&P at 6,604 pricing resolution. (Sources: Epoch Times, Economy Middle East, Trading Economics, Investing.com)

Brent $111.15/bbl (+1.9%), WTI $114.98/bbl (+3.1%) — WTI INVERTED above Brent by $3.83, abnormal (historically Brent at premium). Inversion signals: US SPR at 243M barrels (1984 low), US refinery runs at max, Brent depressed by Hormuz-trapped 'stranded' Middle East supply. Tuesday deadline imminent — binary: deal = $95-100 rapidly; no deal + strikes = $120-130+. Iran rejected 45-day ceasefire. Oil paring gains Monday as ceasefire discussions continue (WTI dropped ~1% intraday to $110.45). Yanbu congestion worsening: 32 supertankers stacked (up from 27), port throughput ~4M bpd vs 7M bpd pipeline = port is binding constraint. Saudi informing customers April allocations reduced. OPEC+ 206K bpd for May — symbolic, cannot be delivered through closed channels. ENR: 'Hormuz bypass infrastructure sized for short disruption. This is not that.' (Sources: OilPriceAPI, Fortune, FinancialContent, CNBC, House of Saud, ENR)

WTI $115.16 > BRENT $111 — INVERSION CONFIRMED [CONFIRMED]. Brent crude ~$111/bbl (+1.4%); WTI $115.16/bbl (+2.4%) — now trading ABOVE Brent. Historically Brent trades at premium to WTI (international vs landlocked US benchmark). The inversion reflects: (1) Strait of Hormuz war premium primarily affecting US-bound shipments via Gulf, (2) Cushing inventory dynamics, (3) US shale supply constraints during the war. WTI > Brent is rare and historically signals US supply tightness — in war context it signals US refiners are bidding aggressively for domestic crude as Gulf imports become harder to secure. Tell for US gasoline price spikes. US crude futures: WTI for May delivery closed at $112.41. Pre-deadline VIX call: analysts expect VIX to spike toward 40 if deadline passes without resolution. TD Securities estimate: nearly 1 BILLION barrels lost by end of month if Strait stays effectively shut to Western shipping (600M crude + 350M refined products) — largest oil supply disruption in history, larger than 1973 or 1979. At $115 WTI / $111 Brent, the market has priced ~$30 of war premium. Full physical disruption justifies $150+. Market still UNDERPRICING tail risk per TD.

EVENING UPDATE: Brent $110.80 (+0.9%), WTI $114.53 (+1.9%) — inversion persists at $3.73 WTI premium. Both paring gains from April 6 (retreated ~1.5%). Classic pre-binary hedging. JPMorgan warned $150+ if Hormuz stays shut into mid-May. Dubai crude at $128.52. Heating oil +5.3% — distillate supply crisis DISTINCT from crude (Hormuz blocks 500K b/d ME diesel, Asia-Europe diesel spread $244/ton). RBOB gasoline -3.8% — demand destruction at $4+/gal, per-capita US consumption at 59-year low. Gasoline demand destruction is self-correcting loop that does NOT exist for diesel. OPEC+ 206K bpd for May conditional on Hormuz — paper only. TD Securities: nearly 1 billion barrels lost by end of April (600M crude + 350M refined). (Sources: CNBC, Fortune, JPMorgan, EIA, OilPrice.com)

BRENT -13.5% TO $94.47 — BIGGEST SINGLE-DAY FALL SINCE 1991 GULF WAR [CONFIRMED]. The ceasefire announcement triggered the largest single-day percentage drop in Brent since January 1991 (Operation Desert Storm air superiority). WTI -15.0% to $96.00. Heating oil -18.2% to $3.66. RBOB gasoline -13.5% to $2.86. The TD Securities '1 billion barrels lost by month-end' tail risk thesis (Bart Melek) directly repriced out. Goldman's $115 April average / 6-week disruption assumption broken. JPMorgan's $150-if-Hormuz-shut-into-mid-May tail risk priced out. CRITICAL CAVEAT (Lloyd's, Axios): physical normalisation will lag the political headline by weeks — war risk insurance still at $10-14M/voyage, ~40 VLCCs still anchored 5-day waits at Yanbu, Sadara $20B JV destroyed yesterday baked in. Fair value is probably $98-105, not $94 and not $115. The structural Iranian 'controlled passage' position remains intact and is the deal's first failure point on Friday April 10. The 'distillate-supply-crisis trade' that yesterday's brief flagged as the most important single signal completely unwound — heating oil's -18.2% is the largest single-day move in any energy benchmark since the war began.

Brent crude crashed 11.5% from $109.27 to $96.75 on April 8 — biggest single-day drop since April 2020 — on 14-day Pakistan-brokered ceasefire announcement. Goldman Sachs risk premium compressed from $14/bbl to $4-6/bbl. BUT THE CRITICAL DISCONNECT: Dated Brent physical spot remained above $120/bbl (CNBC, S&P Global); Dubai physical $126-140/bbl (EBC Financial Group). The $24+ futures-physical gap is the widest since 2008. Amrita Sen (Energy Aspects): futures giving 'false sense of security... the financial market is almost masking the true tightness.' Physical barrels CANNOT move: only 3-4 ships transited Hormuz April 8-9, 40+ VLCCs still anchored at Yanbu with 5-day waits, war risk insurance unchanged at 2.5-5% of vessel value. (Source: Bloomberg, Goldman Sachs, CNBC, S&P Global, EBC Financial Group, Energy Aspects)

Brent bounced to $96.61 (+1.96%) on April 9 as ceasefire euphoria faded. WTI $96.94 (+2.84%). Drivers: (1) Lebanon exclusion undermining ceasefire durability, (2) Iran's Hormuz re-closure confirmed by Bloomberg AIS, (3) only 4 ships through Hormuz on April 9. Jason Schenker (Prestige Economics): 'Almost anything going wrong in these talks could very quickly put us back above $100.' No analyst has revised forecasts — Goldman $85 avg / $71 Q4 and JPM $150 upside scenario remain operative. (Source: Trading Economics, Fortune, CNN, NPR, NBC News)

Brent surged to $98.60/bbl (+4.1%) by late afternoon April 9 — well above the morning $96.61 reading — as ceasefire fracture continued restoring war premium. WTI hit $99.51 (+5.4%), approaching $100 again. WTI-Brent spread near zero/inverted — market reclassifying Gulf crude as semi-stranded, pricing US domestic supply as safer. Heating oil +5.1% to $4.00/gal. Physical-futures gap remains $24+ (Dated Brent $120+ vs futures $98.60), widest since 2008 — physical buyers don't believe ceasefire. Jason Schenker (Prestige Economics): 'Almost anything going wrong in these talks could very quickly put us back above $100.' (Sources: Trading Economics, Fortune, EBC Financial Group, CNBC)

Brent $96.23/bbl (+0.3%, flat). WTI $98.06 (+0.2%). WTI-BRENT INVERSION persists: WTI trading $2 ABOVE Brent — in March Brent averaged $12 above WTI. Inversion signals global supply disruption premium (Brent) deflating on ceasefire while US domestic inflation pressure (WTI) persists. Physical Dated Brent still $120+, Dubai physical $126-140 = $24-44 futures-physical gap (widest since 2008). Chinese tankers halted at Hormuz — toll system intact, ~3% of normal traffic. Stranded vessels revised UP: 800-3,000 (was 479+). Ceasefire has changed PAPER markets but NOT physical reality. March CPI consensus 3.4% = first war-inflation print. Aramco raised Arab Light premiums + cut Asian supply. (Source: yfinance, Bloomberg, NBC News, CNBC, FactSet)

Brent fell -1.5% to $94.45 ahead of Islamabad talks — market positioned for 'dialogue continuation.' Broad energy selloff: WTI -2.3% to $95.63, heating oil -5.0%, gasoline -2.1%. War-trade unwinding: Frontline -1.4%, Exxon -1.6%, Lockheed -1.6%. CRITICAL: Physical-futures gap PERSISTS at $24+ (physical $120+ vs futures $94-96) — widest since 2008. Hormuz at ~3% normal traffic. Paper pricing peace, physical pricing war. Goldman revised Q2 Brent to $90 (from $99), WTI $87 (from $91). Upside if Hormuz stays closed 1 more month: >$100 all 2026 (Q3 $120, Q4 $115). JPM $150 warning now <5 weeks away. Saudi Aramco damage quantified: 600K bpd production lost, East-West Pipeline -700K bpd, Manifa -300K bpd, SATORP refinery SHUT (TotalEnergies confirmed). Arab Light OSP at RECORD $19.50 premium. WTI-Brent inversion widened to ~$3. Like-for-like June: Brent $107.95 vs WTI $96.36 = normal $11.59 premium (headline inversion is contract-timing artifact). March CPI 3.3% ABSORBED — market separated headline from core. If Islamabad fails and oil returns to $110+, CPI 3.3% becomes the FLOOR.

Brent closed Friday at $95.20 (-0.8%). Islamabad talks failed Saturday after 21 hours — Monday repricing expected. During talks, Brent dropped to $87 on Hyperliquid (crypto futures trading 24/7) reflecting hope, then bounced to $92.7 after no-deal confirmed. Monday scenarios: Contained (50%, +$3-5 to $98-100), Ceasefire Wobble (30%, +$5-10 to $100-105), Ceasefire Collapse (20%, +$15-25 to $110-120 toward physical price). Probability-weighted expected move: ~+$5 to ~$100. Physical-futures gap persists ($120+ physical vs $95 futures). Polymarket: >23% odds WTI reaches $105 in April. Goldman Q2 scenario ($100+ all 2026 if Hormuz stays closed another month) now more likely after talks failure. US mine clearing operation launched simultaneously — if successful, could create alternative corridor without Iranian consent, structurally changing the Hormuz supply calculus.

AFTERNOON PRICING UPDATE: Brent $95.20 Friday close represents MAXIMUM DIPLOMATIC HOPE -- markets closed before Islamabad failure confirmed. Hyperliquid (crypto-linked markets that traded over weekend) showed Brent briefly at $87 DURING talks, bouncing to $92.7 on no-deal confirmation -- traditional markets have NOT absorbed this yet. Monday repricing expected +$3-10 to $98-105 range. Physical-futures gap persists ($120+ physical vs $95.20 futures = $25+ widest since 2008). Goldman Q2 scenario ($100+ all 2026 if Hormuz stays closed another month) now MORE LIKELY after talks failure. WTI at $96.57 (+10.7% monthly). Heating oil -4.5% daily -- war premium in refined products deflating faster than crude (seasonal crack spread collapse from war-inflated $1.42/gal). Mine clearing introduces new variable: if commercial corridor established by late April per McNally thesis, structurally undermines Iran's Hormuz leverage and oil floor. (Sources: market data, Hyperliquid via market participants, Goldman Sachs)

Brent surged +7.4% to $102.20 on US naval blockade announcement. WTI +8.4% to $104.73. Heating oil +8.7% to $4.09 — refined products outpacing crude, signaling refining capacity stress beyond just crude supply. CENTCOM confirmed blockade of all Iranian ports effective April 13 10:00 AM ET. Iran's ~1.3M bpd remaining exports under direct interdiction threat. OPEC+ 206K bpd May increase is moot — most Gulf barrels stranded behind Hormuz. JP Morgan pre-blockade: $120 if stalemate drags to July; $150 worst case. Goldman's ceasefire-optimistic $90 Q2 forecast already obsolete. (Sources: yfinance confirmed, CNBC, Al Jazeera, CENTCOM)

Oil PARTIALLY REVERSED Sunday's blockade spike — sell-off reversed in afternoon. Brent initially fell from $103+ toward $97-98 on Trump's CLAIMED (unverified by Iran) statement that 'Iran reached out,' then crept back to $99.19 by evening close (-0.2% on day). The afternoon creep-back toward $100 signals market remains nervous about Hormuz supply reality even as it priced 'deal in days' earlier. WTI similarly recovered. The earlier intraday reversal was driven entirely by Trump's unverified claim and AP reporting of possible Thursday talks via Pakistan/Turkey/Egypt facilitation. Wall Street repriced: 'blockade = coercion tool forcing deal' NOT 'blockade = escalation.' RHETORIC-DRIVEN move — blockade remains operational, war risk insurance at prohibitive prices (not suspended per Lloyd's List evening correction). $99.19 approaches the $100 trigger; any disruption to deal narrative restores $105+ instantly. (Sources: Bloomberg, CNBC, BNN Bloomberg, TradingEconomics — 4 independent sources)

Physical-futures gap near widest of entire crisis: Dated Brent (physical, real barrels) at $120+ vs Brent futures at $99.19 (evening close) = $21 gap. Physical market players (refiners, traders with actual ships) are paying $120+ per barrel while futures traders (Wall Street, algos) are paying ~$99. One of these is wrong. The gap reflects: insurance at prohibitive prices (commercial ships deterred by cost even though coverage technically available), blockade operational (31 vessels/24hrs), Hormuz NOT reopened. The futures market is pricing a deal that does not yet exist. (Sources: market data, Goldman Sachs, S&P Global)

EVENING CORRECTION on war risk insurance: Lloyd's List clarifies P&I clubs have NOT cancelled war risk cover. Coverage IS available but at prohibitive prices — 2.5-5% of hull value ($10-14M per VLCC trip). LMA: 88% of Lloyd's marine war market participants continue to have appetite to underwrite. The constraint is PRICE not AVAILABILITY — de facto barrier for most commercial operators. Since insurers have capacity, a diplomatic breakthrough could normalize premiums in weeks rather than months. US DFC $40B reinsurance facility attempting to fill the pricing gap. This remains a critical oil-flow constraint, but recovery timeline is shorter than previously assessed. (Sources: Lloyd's List, CNBC, Kennedys Law — 3 independent sources, April 14 evening)

Saudi Arabia's East-West oil pipeline restored to full capacity (~7M bpd), diverting oil to Yanbu — a critical workaround for the Hormuz blockade that reduces the blockade's effective impact on total Gulf oil supply reaching global markets. This is a secondary contributor (~5%) to oil's reversal, behind the Trump 'deal' claim (~95%). (Sources: Al Jazeera, CS Monitor — 2 independent sources)

Analyst consensus: Goldman Sachs says another month of Hormuz closure means Brent averages above $100 for all of 2026. JP Morgan warns of $150 overshoot if Hormuz stays shut into mid-May. Bob McNally (Rapidan Energy): mine clearing then blockade then commercial escort by late April — 'I don't expect this ceasefire to last.' Oil sell-off was 'unwarranted.' The current $97.98 is pricing 'deal in days' — highly reversible if Thursday talks don't materialize or IRGC-US naval incident occurs. (Sources: Goldman Sachs, JP Morgan, Rapidan Energy via Fortune — 3 independent sources)

Brent crude fell to $95.21/bbl (from $99.19), a ~4% decline in a single session — second consecutive session below $100. Trading range $94.43-$99.41 reflecting extreme intraday volatility. The decline driven by: (1) Trump's 'next two days' talks claim amplifying deal hopes, (2) CENTCOM's 'fully implemented' blockade claim paradoxically reassuring markets, (3) Israel-Lebanon talks creating broader de-escalation narrative. CRITICAL: Dated Brent (physical barrels) hit $144/bbl peak approximately April 7-11 per Al Jazeera citing Platts/Argus assessments — physical-futures gap exploded to ~$49, more than DOUBLE the $22 gap reported yesterday. Morgan Stanley: 'much more violent shock in physical Brent-linked barrels compared to financial contract.' Physical market pricing blockade reality ($10-14M insurance, crew safety); futures pricing 'deal within days.' One of these markets is profoundly wrong. (Sources: TradingEconomics, Investing.com, Al Jazeera citing Platts/Argus, Morgan Stanley, CNBC)

EVENING UPDATE: Brent closed at $95.78 (+1.0%), second consecutive session below $100. THREE-LAYER OIL DISCONNECTION deepening — the widest of the entire war: (1) Physical Dated Brent $120-144 (what refiners/tankers actually pay), (2) Brent futures $95.78 (Wall Street), (3) Equity-implied Brent ~$85-90 (based on XLE -2.0% while S&P at new post-war high 6,967). These cannot all be right — reconvergence will be violent. Energy sector rout: XLE -2.0%, Exxon -2.2%, Heating Oil -2.2%, Gasoline -2.2%. Market pricing 'deal within days' built on Trump's UNVERIFIED claim. If talks don't materialize by Thursday, entire sector reverses +3-5%. (Sources: yfinance confirmed, TradingEconomics, CNBC, Bloomberg)

Day 50. BRENT -6.5% TO $92.92 — LARGEST SINGLE-DAY DROP SINCE APR 8 CEASEFIRE [CONFIRMED]. Multi-factor convergence: (1) 10-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire announced Apr 16 (Trump, State Dept), (2) Trump 'very close to over' on ABC News + Munir Tehran shuttle confirms active Round 2 diplomacy, (3) Goldman cut Q2 to $90 (institutional anchor below current), (4) Market pricing >50% deal probability before Apr 21 expiry. COUNTER-SIGNAL: EIA inventories -9.13M bbl vs -154K expected = strongly bullish fundamentals IGNORED by deal-sentiment market. Physical Dated Brent at ~$132 means physical-futures gap WIDENED to ~$39 (from ~$35) — futures pricing deal, physical pricing blockade. They cannot both be right. CF Industries +3.2% COUNTER-DIRECTIONAL confirms physical supply disruption thesis persists. (Sources: yfinance CONFIRMED, Goldman via Reuters, EIA, Fortune, CNBC)

EVENING UPDATE: Brent settled $96.16 (-3.3% net) vs morning -6.5% snapshot at $92.92 — afternoon reversal of +3.5% driven by (a) US session opening and Pakistani-flagged Shalamar (~440K bbl to Karachi) confirmed Hormuz transit during afternoon reinforcing selective-transit regime (JCS Caine 'port blockade not strait closure'), and (b) futures recognizing EIA -9.13M bbl draw ignored at morning. WTI DIVERGED the other way — settled $87.77 (-7.3%), a canonical oil-complex break from Brent that widened the Brent-WTI spread to $8.39, the widest since the blockade began April 13. The mechanism: Brent (global/Gulf-delivery) is held up by Dated Brent $132 physical, Hormuz port-blockade, 0.8-1.0% hull insurance; WTI (US domestic) is freer to price deal-optimism without a shipping-route hedge. Physical Dated Brent NOT re-quoted (Good Friday) — gap narrowed to ~$36 only because futures bounced, not because physical eased. Heating Oil -8.7% (largest single-asset move today) = distillate-supply-crisis trade fully unwound. US equity/VIX/10Y CLOSED Good Friday — the S&P 7,041 ATH roll unchanged into Monday's 72-hour weekend re-price that includes Sunday April 19 waiver expiry. [CONFIRMED] (Sources: yfinance BZ=F/CL=F, CNN liveblog, TradingEconomics TTF, EIA)

CORRECTED FRIDAY SETTLE: Brent $91.87/bbl -7.6% (yfinance BZ=F continuous); wire-ICE print $90.38 -9.1%. WTI $84.00 -11.3%. Heating Oil $3.34 -12.8% (largest single-asset move). RBOB $2.95 -6.9%. Evening brief's $96.16 was the CNN 4pm intraday mark, NOT the actual settle; its framing of 'US markets CLOSED Good Friday' was wrong (Good Friday 2026 was April 3; April 17 was a full regular session). S&P 500 closed at record high 7,126.06 (+1.2%). Brent-WTI spread actually $7.87 at close (not $8.39 stale). Intraday Brent low $87.94 (-11.5%). Araghchi Hormuz-open X-post (~17:00 EST) was the single catalyst for the largest single-day crude move of the crisis. Intraday bounce from $87.94 to $91.87 reflects Trump's 'blockade in full force' response + Baghaei's 'reciprocal measures' conditional — market priced the announcement as probability-weighted, not unconditional. Physical Dated Brent still ~$132 (Thursday last-print; no public Friday quote located); physical-futures gap WIDENS to ~$40, widest of war. Goldman Q2 $90 base case validated within $2; Morgan Stanley $110 Q2 is $20 above market. Analyst divergence is model divergence on whether Araghchi declaration is structural or conditional. (Sources: yfinance BZ=F/CL=F/HO=F/RB=F, Washington Times, NBC News, Motley Fool, PBS News, Argus/Platts for Dated Brent Thursday print)

SUNDAY RE-PRICING SETUP [Day 52, T-hours to Monday Globex]. Three fresh intel catalysts argue Friday's -9.1% crash should reverse more than Saturday's base case assumed: (1) Iran's Supreme National Security Council (SNSC) — the civilian-apex body chaired by President Pezeshkian that by statute outranks the IRGC and Foreign Ministry — formally ratified the IRGC Hormuz closure on Saturday evening IST, re-imposing the $1/bbl loaded-tanker fee framework and declaring Araghchi's Friday 'completely open' declaration suspended until the US lifts its blockade. Civilian/IRGC/Supreme Leader three-way split operationally collapsed. (2) Kinetic broadened beyond tanker-only: container ship projectile damage + Malta-flagged *Mein Schiff 4* IRGC VHF threat. (3) India MEA summoned Iran's Ambassador — first formal India-Iran diplomatic friction of crisis; demand-side absorber structurally weakening. Eurasia Group's Gregory Brew: 'Iran now holds effective control over the critical oil chokepoint,' few if any ships while dueling blockades stand; US gasoline $5/gal by summer. Monday probability distribution per markets.md C1: Full re-pricing $100+ (35%, +5 vs Sat eve), Base bounce $95-98 (45%, unchanged), Muted $92-94 (15%, -10), Continued unwind $90-92 (5%, +5). 10pp shift out of 'muted bounce' reflects SNSC formalisation; small right-tail kept live by Trump's 'productive conversations' off-ramp. Goldman-vs-MS Monday falsifiable test: Goldman right if Brent opens within 2% of $91.87 (paper prices kinetic as one-off); MS right if Brent gaps up 3%+ on Sunday Globex 22:00 GMT open (paper converges to physical). CENTCOM non-response (16+ hours) remains binding on 'Iran executes reciprocal measures' trigger upgrade. (Sources: intel.md A1/A2/A3/B3, markets.md C1, Press TV, Tasnim, Washington Examiner, Al Jazeera, GZERO Media, CNBC)

SUNDAY GLOBEX BRENT INDICATIVELY ~$98 — MORNING +5-8% RE-OPEN THESIS VINDICATED AT UPPER EDGE [REPORTED via Investing.com Sunday quote + specialist aggregator]. Sunday Globex (CME ICE) indicatively ~$98 for June Brent = +8.4% over Friday wire-ICE $90.38 / +6.7% over yfinance $91.87. Morning brief base case: +5-8% -> $93-97.50; severe tail: $100+. Sunday print sits ON THE SEAM between base and severe cases — i.e., escalation pricing at upper edge of 'standard brinkmanship' band but not at the tail. The 'Sunday quiet' non-event (no fresh IRGC fire) provides a CEILING — $100+ would have required Sunday-kinetic repeat. Paper-physical gap closing BY PAPER RISING toward physical (Dated ~$132 Thursday), not physical falling — direction the morning brief argued. WTI Globex indicatively ~$89-90 (consistent +8%). Lloyd's AWRP no Sunday republish; Monday London open is next hard-money tell. If AWRP steps to 1.5%+ hull at London open, Brent prints $100+; if AWRP holds 1.0-1.2%, Brent consolidates $96-98. Goldman ($90 Q2 base / $115 Q4 severe Apr 15 note): paper already converging upward to physical at Sunday Globex; Goldman base case visibly under pressure within 4 days. Morgan Stanley ($110 Q2): vindicated on direction — confirmed if Brent above $100 within 10 sessions.

BRENT MONDAY ASIA CASH ~$95.42 INTRADAY (+7.9% vs FRIDAY $90.38 WIRE / +3.9% vs $91.87 yfinance) — GOLDMAN ONE-OFF CEILING BREACHED; MS FULL-CONVERGENCE NOT YET [CONFIRMED via Bloomberg, Fortune, Axios]. Sunday Globex printed $98 indicative; Monday Asia cash retraced to ~$95.42. Sits $5 below MS $100 convergence trigger; above Goldman $96-98 interim band ceiling (suggests Globex $98 was emotional overshoot, Touska treated as escalatory-not-supply-disruptive). Five-catalyst repricing stack: (1) SNSC Saturday ratification (transit cert + $1/bbl fee + civilian apex alignment), (2) Saturday IRGC kinetic broadening across VLCC/container/cruise classes, (3) USS Spruance Touska seizure Sunday, (4) IRNA Round 2 rejection Sunday, (5) Bessent Iranian waiver elapsed 05:30 IST (Russian RENEWED). Reversibility HIGH on Touska absorption + Islamabad delegation landed = $92-93 within a week; upside tail $103-110 within 3 sessions if IRGC kinetic on US Navy by Tuesday 23:59 IST.

BESSENT IRANIAN-OIL WAIVER ELAPSED 05:30 IST MONDAY; NO LAST-MINUTE EXTENSION — RUSSIAN-OIL WAIVER RENEWED (ASYMMETRIC DESIGN) [CONFIRMED ACTION]. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent's April 15 public non-renewal declaration ("we will not be renewing the general license on Russian oil, and we will not be renewing the general license on Iranian oil") executed at 05:30 IST Monday on the Iran side — no extension, carve-out, or wind-down. Incremental Iranian supply to Indian refiners structurally zero from 05:30 IST onward. NUANCE versus Friday framing: the Russian waiver DID end up being RENEWED (30-day extension for India) despite Bessent's initial "neither" statement — The Week, Business Today, The Federal all confirm the Russian-side reversal. India's ~1.9 mbpd March Russian flow preserved. This asymmetry — Iranian non-renewal executed, Russian renewal executed — is a deliberate US policy design separating energy supply security (Russian oil continues to cushion global balance, particularly for India) from maximum-pressure sanctions on Iran's regime.

LLOYD'S AWRP ~1% HULL — NO SUNDAY REPUBLISH; MONDAY LONDON OPEN IS NEXT HARD TELL [REPORTED]. Lloyd's AWRP held ~1% hull through Friday despite Saturday IRGC kinetic — insurers did not step UP over the weekend. Evening brief expected Monday step to 1.5%+; morning-side data (Lloyd's List, Reinsurance News, LMA) suggests broader AWRP trend is MODERATION, with certain transits eased to 0.8-1.0% hull. Expected Monday move +20-80bps (milder than evening's +50-150bps). If Lloyd's republish prints above 1.5%, Goldman severe-tail ($115 Q4) moves to base; if holds at 1.0%, paper-traders pricing a step-up are disappointed. Crisis peak was 2.5% (60x pre-crisis 0.025-0.04%); current ~32-40x pre-war multiplier — elevated but easing.

$20B FROZEN-FUNDS-FOR-URANIUM WIRE CLAIM — TREAT AS CLAIMED, POTENTIAL SEED-AND-AMPLIFY PATTERN [REPORTED wire-chain, anonymous sources]. Multiple oil-market wires reported Fri/Sat that "the US may release $20 billion in frozen Iranian funds in exchange for enriched uranium stockpiles" (cited by TradingEconomics, specialist aggregators). First specific number attached to frozen-funds concession in current round — prior framework was $6B from 2023 Biden-era hostage deal plus South Korean/Iraqi holdings. Single-chain reporting with respect to the specific $20B, attributed to "sources" without named US/Iran official. Classic seed-and-amplify risk pattern. Treat CLAIMED pending named-sourced confirmation. Either Washington is offering more than prior rounds (so IRNA rejection inconsistent) or the $20B figure was leaked by US to make Iran refusal look unreasonable (information operation).

MONDAY CASH CLOSE $94.88 (+5.0% 1D, +46.1% SINCE FEB 28) — BELOW GOLDMAN $96-98 INTERIM BAND DESPITE 88% HORMUZ TRANSIT SUPPRESSION [CONFIRMED MARKET DATA; paper-physical divergence is the signal]. WTI $86.50 (+3.2% 1D); spread $8.38. Primary chain (crisis ~4.7% of 5.0%): *Touska* seizure Sunday → SNSC blockade ratification (Sat) → Hormuz transit Sunday ZERO + Monday ~16 vs 138 (88% suppression, MarineTraffic AIS, The National, CNBC) → Trump Bloomberg 'highly unlikely' extension + 'blockade stays until deal is signed' (6-source) → risk premium re-bid on paper. Secondary (~0.3%): Bessent Iranian waiver elapsed 05:30 IST Monday (Russian RENEWED — asymmetric). Paper market NOT confirming 88% transit-suppression — physical reality says disruption is at severe-case levels, paper prices as contained one-off. Wednesday binary resolves: renewal → $91-93; lapse → $100-105 within 48 hrs. Reversibility HIGH for crisis component on Wed renewal; structural floor $92-93 per Brew/Eurasia Group absent full diplomatic resolution.

ANALYST CONSENSUS TUESDAY — GOLDMAN-MS-BREW FRAMEWORKS CONVERGE ON WEDNESDAY-BINARY [ASSESSMENTS]. Morgan Stanley (Q2 $110, Q3 $100): Tuesday paper $94.69-95.50 is at lower edge of Monday settle — paper NOT confirming physical distress despite 88% transit-collapse; Wednesday renewal → retrace to $91-93, Wednesday lapse → $100-105 within 48 hrs. Goldman (Q2 $90 base / severe $115 Q4): 88% suppression IS the severe-case scenario the $115 Q4 prices; asymmetry resolves only through Wednesday — lapse → $115 Q4 base within 3 sessions, renewal → $85-90 band reasserts. Brew/Eurasia (floor $92-95): Brent below $80 'very unlikely' at any point 2026; Monday transit data + Trump blockade-stays posture sets structural floor $92-95; ceasefire real economic effect was AWRP moderation — already achieved — incremental diplomatic progress delivers diminishing returns on Brent. Falsifiable: Brent below $90 within 30 days post-April 22 = Brew wrong on floor; Brent above $100 within 10 sessions = MS direction correct.

BRENT OVERNIGHT ROUND-TRIP: $95.48 TUE SETTLE → $101.97 (01:26 IST WED SPIKE, +7%) → $92.48 (11:51 IST WED) [CONFIRMED, multi-source]. Arc: Tuesday European close $90.38 → Tuesday NYMEX US settle ~$95.48-95.75 (US session recovered on Vance-Pakistan-trip-paused fear) → 01:26 IST Wed spike to $101.97 (+7.03%) → Trump extension → Tokyo morning $98.48 → 11:51 IST Wed $92.48. Morgan Stanley $100 convergence trigger fired briefly; extension unfired it. CRITICAL GRAPH STATE: Brent $92.48 is now BELOW pre-Touska-seizure Monday ($94.88) WHILE Hormuz physical transit remains at virtual standstill (Tasnim: 'Iran will not reopen the Strait' while blockade persists). The paper-physical divergence yesterday flagged has REVERSED DIRECTION — paper now UNDERPRICES physical disruption by ~$5-7/bbl. Reversibility VERY high — Mojtaba rejection would drive Brent back to $97-100 within 48 hours.

edges.context: Day 54. Monday cash $94.88 (+5.0%) below Goldman $96-98 band despite 88% transit suppression (16 vs 138) + Sunday ZERO-transit. Paper-physical divergence LOCKED IN as intelligence signal — operational severe-case + paper priced as contained one-off. Floating-storage Gulf + AWRP ~1.0% + Wednesday-binary pricing suppressing paper re-rate. Wednesday April 22 resolves: renewal → $91-93 band; lapse → $100-105 within 48 hrs. Weight reflects operational enforcement; divergence is a regime-state, not weight-increase trigger.

Day 52 / waiver T-0 (Sunday midnight UTC / 05:30 IST Monday). Bessent confirmed no renewal of Iranian waiver; separate 30-day Russian waiver extension carved out. IOC Jaya 2M bbl discharged; Reliance Felicity 2M bbl at Sikka discharged; Reliance rejected Derya + Lenore Saturday on compliance grounds. India MEA summoned Iran's Ambassador Saturday evening — first formal démarche of crisis. OMC daily USD demand $250-300M becomes binding on spot rupee at Brent $95+ Monday. Supply recalibration to Iraq/UAE/Saudi/Africa/US/Latin America is Q2-Q3 structural theme. Confirmed-action weight shift +2.0.

Day 51. Brent $91.87 (-7.6%) drove rupee to Rs92.57 (-0.9%, rupee stronger). Brent crash mechanical for INR relief; structural stress continues via FPI outflows (Rs 48,213 cr first 10 days April) and Monday's waiver double-door closure. 93.50 trigger intact.

Day 47. Oil at $99.19 (evening close, crept back from intraday ~$97-98) — approaches $100 again. Hike probability >50% for year-end (CME FedWatch). CPI 3.3% treated as transitory but imported inflation rising. Fed trapped: oil-driven inflation prevents cuts, growth deterioration prevents hikes. If deal fails and oil returns to $105+, inflation expectations ratchet permanently higher.

Day 51. S&P record close 7,126.06 (+1.2%) on SAME Hormuz-open catalyst that crashed Brent. Oil -7.6% → energy-inflation tail-risk reduced → 10Y -1.5% to 4.25% → risk-on rotation. XLE -2.8% decoupled from index's +1.2% rally — sector transition from war premium to de-escalation pricing. VIX 17.48 war-era low. Earnings component ~0.2% (Pepsi beat, Schwab mixed); Fed path unchanged. Crisis component (~1.0% of 1.2%) round-trips if Monday Islamabad fails.

OPEC+ April 5 meeting is next binary event. 206K bpd increase confirmed for April. Decision on May levels could accelerate or restrain supply response. Most barrels still stranded if Hormuz stays shut.
Oil and gas prices linked. LNG contracts often indexed to oil. Dual pressure on energy importers.
Oil at $109 post-address = India inflation = RBI can't cut. RBI MPC April 6-8 must decide with oil back near pre-crash levels. India 10Y at 7.00-7.04%. Stagflation trap deepens.

trigger_points.mechanism: India's import bill rises ~$25B annually per $10 oil increase. Sustained $100+ oil widens CAD to ~3.5% GDP.

Brent was above $100 for ~13 days (March 20 - April 2), broke below during ceasefire, then surged back to $102.20 on blockade announcement (April 13). Now reversed to $97.98 on April 14 — one session below $100 driven by Trump's unverified 'Iran reached out' claim. The trigger was previously CROSSED and sustained $100+ for weeks. A rhetoric-driven single-session dip below $100 does not deactivate the structural condition — Hormuz remains blocked, insurance suspended, physical Dated Brent at $120+. If oil returns above $100 (highly likely if deal claims are unverified or IRGC incident occurs), sustained count resumes. | UPDATE 2026-04-14: Keep at WATCHING — one session below $100 on unverified claim is not sufficient to declare the trigger deactivated. Physical market reality ($120+) contradicts futures pricing ($98).
Would signal supply normalization. India relief: lower import bill, rupee stabilization, room for RBI rate cuts.

trigger_points.condition: Brent spikes above $130 on Houthi Red Sea entry
trigger_points.mechanism: Double chokepoint (Hormuz + Red Sea) would remove majority of Gulf export capacity. Would trigger emergency SPR releases, possible recession.

trigger_points.condition: Brent-Hormuz paper-physical divergence >$10/bbl in EITHER direction sustained 3 sessions
trigger_points.mechanism: Paper Brent (futures settle) vs dated Brent or physical-disruption-implied fair value. Direction reversed Wednesday morning: paper $92.48, physical-implied value given Hormuz standstill ~$98-100. Either direction sustained 3 sessions = trigger. Replaces previous one-sided divergence framing.
